data.title: A whale (a large investor) reopened a long position of 20,000 ETH using 3x leverage, making a total profit of over $28 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, after closing its ETH position with a profit of $4.7 million, the whale pension-usdt.eth (0x0ddf...a902) reopened a long position in ETH using 3x leverage, holding 20,000 ETH, worth $67 million. Currently, the whale has a floating profit of $1.2 million, and its total profit has exceeded $28 million.

data.title: Lighter introduces LIT staking, which unlocks four permissions including LLP staking benefits and fee discounts.
data.description: Lighter has announced the launch of its LIT staking feature, offering the following benefits: 1. Users staking LIT will gain access to LLP (Lighter Liquidity Pools). For every 1 LIT staked, 10 USDC will be immediately deposited into LLP. Existing LLP holders have a two-week grace period (ending January 28th) during which they can retain their existing funds. After this period, continued LIT staking is required to participate in LLP. 2. The tiered fees charged by market makers and high-frequency trading firms will be adjusted within two weeks. Overall fees will increase, but LIT staking will offer a fee discount, maintaining the lowest fee tier at its current level. We will release the detailed tiered fee rules several days in advance to allow trading firms to adjust their algorithms. 3. Staking LIT will generate returns; the annualized yield will be published immediately after the feature takes effect. 4. Staking 100 LIT will enjoy zero withdrawal and transfer fees.

data.title: Solana Mobile has opened up SKR airdrop eligibility information; claims will be available and staking will be supported starting January 21st.
data.description: According to an official Solana Mobile X account post, Solana Mobile has disclosed the specific allocation details of the SKR airdrop. A total of nearly 2 billion SKR were distributed to the community, with approximately 1.82 billion allocated to 100,908 users and approximately 141 million to 188 developers, covering both user and developer groups. The official announcement stated that SKR will be officially available for collection on January 21st at 02:00 (UTC). Eligible participants can check their allocation and corresponding tier in advance and need to prepare a small amount of SOL to cover on-chain transaction fees incurred during collection. After collection, SKR can be used for staking to earn rewards. Furthermore, Solana Mobile confirmed that Season 2 has also started simultaneously, and related ecosystem incentive programs are ongoing.

data.title: Sol Meme (Buttcoin) surged over 260% intraday, pushing its market capitalization above $20 million.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the market capitalization of Buttcoin, the Meme coin within the Solana ecosystem, surged by over 260% intraday, briefly exceeding $20 million, before settling back to approximately $16.94 million. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: Federal Reserve Beige Book Indicates Modest Economic Growth Across U.S.
data.description:
According to PANews, the Federal Reserve's Beige Book reveals that economic activity in eight out of the twelve Federal Reserve districts experienced slight to moderate growth. Three districts reported no change, while one district noted a moderate decline. This marks an improvement compared to the previous three reporting periods, where most districts reported little change in economic activity.

The Beige Book indicates a slightly optimistic outlook for future activities, with most districts anticipating modest to moderate growth in the coming months. Consumer spending saw slight to moderate growth in most banks, largely due to the holiday shopping season. Employment conditions remained largely unchanged, with eight districts reporting no change in hiring activities.

Prices grew at a moderate pace in most districts, with only two districts reporting slight price increases. Tariff-induced cost pressures were a common issue across all districts. As pre-tariff inventories deplete, businesses are passing additional costs onto consumers, hinting at emerging inflationary pressures.

data.title: Sui Network Resumes Normal Operations After Temporary Disruption
data.description:
According to PANews, Sui announced at 05:24 on January 15 that its network has resumed full operations and transactions are proceeding normally. Users experiencing issues are advised to refresh their applications or browser windows. A comprehensive review of the incident will be shared in the coming days.

Previously, at 11:24 the previous evening, the Sui mainnet experienced a network halt.

data.title: Sui Blockchain Restores Operations After Network Outage
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the layer-1 Sui blockchain has resumed normal operations following a six-hour network outage that disrupted transactions on the high-speed network. The Sui Foundation announced that transactions are now proceeding as expected and advised users experiencing issues to refresh their applications or browser windows. This update was shared on the social media platform X on Wednesday.

The Sui Foundation confirmed the outage at 3:24 pm UTC on Wednesday, informing its 1.1 million followers that core developers were actively addressing the issue. However, the foundation has not provided details on the cause of the 'Consensus outage,' which affected over $1 billion in value on the chain and halted user transactions. The investigation into the problem began at 2:52 pm UTC, and the issue was resolved by 8:44 pm UTC, restoring the network after nearly six hours of downtime.

This incident marks the second significant outage for the Sui blockchain since its launch in May 2023, with a previous disruption occurring in November 2024. Comparatively, Solana, another high-speed blockchain, has experienced similar challenges but has not faced any outages in the past 18 months. Solana has implemented emergency updates in the past to enable validators to better coordinate and address critical client-side issues. Recently, the Solana Status X account urged validators to upgrade to a new version containing essential patches.

In the wake of the network outage, the price of Sui's (SUI) token experienced a brief 4% increase before stabilizing around $1.84, according to CoinGecko data. Despite the temporary spike, the token's price has remained relatively stable since the Sui Foundation confirmed the outage.

data.title: Senate encryption bill enters critical phase, with a surge in amendments and intensifying lobbying efforts.
data.description: With the Senate Banking Committee hearing approaching, US crypto legislation has entered its final sprint. The bill has now seen over 70 amendments, with disagreements rapidly escalating over stablecoin yields and DeFi regulation, drawing involvement from the crypto industry, banking lobbying groups, and consumer protection organizations. The Senate will vote on the amendments on Thursday. The bill aims to clarify the regulatory boundaries between the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), define the nature of digital assets, and introduce new disclosure requirements. Committee Chairman Tim Scott released the 278-page bill text on Monday, followed by numerous amendments from both parties. Some proposals involve granting the Treasury Department the power to sanction "distributed application layers," while others focus on stablecoin yields, which have become the biggest point of contention. Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that his Stand With Crypto group will score Thursday's amendment vote, saying it will test whether senators are "on the side of bank profits or on the side of consumer rewards." Industry insiders point out that while the bill still has momentum, its final direction remains highly uncertain.

data.title: Zcash Foundation: The SEC has closed its investigation and will not take enforcement action.
data.description: The Zcash Foundation stated that the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has formally concluded its multi-year investigation into it and explicitly stated that it will not recommend any enforcement action. The foundation said it received a subpoena related to "certain crypto asset offerings" in August 2023 and has now received notification of the case's closure from the regulator. An SEC spokesperson responded that the agency "does not comment on the existence of investigations." This closure comes against the backdrop of a shift in the SEC's enforcement approach towards the crypto industry. Over the past year, the SEC has dropped or terminated several cases, including one involving Coinbase, and investigations into some DeFi projects, a move seen as a contrast to its previously more aggressive regulatory stance.
